text: I want to thank the Secretary for the comments provided on this matter. Climate change and the reduction of greenhouse gas emission for international shipping remain at the forefront of Tunisia's priority. The Tunisia delegation expressed our strong support for the establishment of a subcommittee on reduction of DHE emissions from ships. This proposal will enhance our effectiveness in addressing these critical challenges, and we are particularly encouraged that this arrangement will promote a more inclusive and multilingual approach to our work. Multilingualism is essential to us. It ensures that all member state regardless of their linguistic backgrounds can participate meaningfully and contribute their expertise to these vital discussions. We believe this subcommittee will strengthen our institutional capacity and enable us to make meaningful progress on maritime emission reduction in a manner that truly affects the diversity of our organization. Thank you, Chair. Thank you, Tunisia. Somalia, please. Thank you, Chairman. Our delegation would like to express their condolences to the Republic of Kenya on the Bati and DHEA Prime Minister. Our delegation would like to support the proposal of establishment of subcommittee in order to effectively adopt the net zero framework establishing a subcommittee is crucial for coordinating if for this ensuring comprehensive implementation. Subcommittee can bring together expertise in various fields related to climate action ensuring that framework is formed by the best practices. Somalia recognized the need for indicated GAGA committee to manage effectively the task associated with zero net zero framework for shipping. Thank you, Chairman. Thank you, Somalia. Canada, please. Chair, the Canadian delegation would like to extend its condolences to our colleagues from Kenya for the passing of their former Prime Minister. We'd also like to thank France for hosting the reception last night. Okay, Bo. Distinguished delegates, good morning and happy Thursday. Chair, we have listened with the tension and interest to the many helpful interventions. This matter touches linguistic, administrative and economic considerations that will need ongoing and careful consideration. In the meantime, we would like to align with remarks of the United Kingdom which we feel provide an effective way forward. Thank you, Chair. Thank you, Canada. By the gas, please. Thank you, Mr. President. We would like to express our condolences to the Republic of Kenya on the passing of their former Prime Minister. We thank all delegations who have provided coffee, breaks and reception up until now and those of you who will do so in the coming days. We would like to thank the voluntary multi-dern trust fund to have made it possible for us to participate in this session. We thank the co-submitters of the document and also the Secretary at for the clarifications they have provided on the administrative and budgetary implications. The delegation of the gas to support the proposal, we believe that this will speed up our negotiations and the technical debates on this matter. We would like to bear in mind the need to ensure the active participation and photo-participation of all delegations in the discussion. Thank you. Thank you, Chair. Good morning to all delegates. Firstly, this delegation sends our sincere condolences to the delegation of Kenya in passing of the former Prime Minister. Also, we would like to appreciate the delegations who have provided the generous progressments and reception during the past days. Chair, I think we would like to support the principle in establishing a dedicated subcommittee for the nature of framework to exit which goals. However, this delegation would like to support the intervention made by Tuvalu to be collectively considered. Thank you, Chair. Firstly, say, shows would like to pass condolences to Kenya for their loss. On the matter of hand, though we are not opposed to a subcommittee due to the complexity of work ahead and see the benefits that a subcommittee would bring in ensuring the effective implementation of the GHE strategy, we are in support of Tuvalu in regard to the our concerns that a subcommittee will have on the burden of states. As such, a thorough analysis on the cost impact needs to be carried out to ensure the cost of a subcommittee does not become a burden on the states. Thank you very much, Chair. Thank you, Mr. Shells. Mozang, please. Thank you very much, Mr. Chair. First of all, Mozang, we would like to align itself with other delegates, as sending our health condolences for the passing of the former Minister of the Republic of Kenya. And also we would like to thank the Moderna Fund for providing means for our colleague to attend this session. On the matter of hand, Chair Mozang, we are aligned with other member states on the importance of adopting the creation of the subcommittee which will provide grounds for us to depend and exchange on deliberations on these G&G mission strategy. Thank you very much, Chair. Thank you, Mozang Bic. Libya, please. Thank you very much, Chair. I would like to also align itself with those who have taken the floor to express condolences to Kenya. We also thank Egypt, Kenya, Mexico and all the other delegations who have supported this document to clear that the tendency here in the committee to accept the proposal, there are many advantages to this, to have a specialisation in a subcommittee for these discussions, but there are other matters which have been raised in particular. The digital matters, financial implications, linguistic implications. We believe that this proposal should be accepted. Thank you, Chair. Thank you, Mozang Bic. Libya, rather. Palau, please. Thank you, Chair, to give me the floor. I'm going to everybody. Very brief. I would like to express our condolences for the history of the delegation of Kenya. Back to the matter in Andrew, I would like to support the intervention made by Tuvalo, supported by many others, as well as with the latter to subject to thank consideration so the intervention made by the Marshal Islander. Thank you. Thank you, Palau. Chamonix, please. Thank you, Chair. Good morning, everyone. Firstly, we would like to also express our sincere condolences to the delegation of Kenya. Turning to the matter of hand. We would like to align ourselves with the statements made by Mozang, Greece, and others, and we would also like to thank the UK for their proposal for a way forward. We can support this approach. Thank you, Chair. Thank you, Chamonix. Thank you. Hi,<|hu|> I would like to thank the delegation of Kenya for the passing of the aforementioned Prime Minister. Kiribas learned that he is the father of democracy. Chair, with that being said, Kiribas aligns with our calling from Tuvalo, Papua Nukinia and others. With the establishment of the subcommittee on the greenhouse gas reduction, we respectfully request consideration for expanded access to the multi-donut trust fund so that all the seats like Kiribas can engage fully and consistently in their committees. Thank you, Chair. Thank you, Kiribas. I seek Kenya as requested in the following, again, Kenya, please. Thank you, Chair. We would like to most sincerely appreciate all the delegations who have expressed their condolences to us on the passing of our former Prime Minister, the right Honorable Raihla Amolo Odinga. It's indeed an immeasurable loss for Kenya, for Africa, for all of us. It passed on yesterday morning and our country has been thrown into unprecedented morning. On the wall, Raihla was the most consequential leader in the history of Kenya. It dedicated his entire adult life to the service of our nation. The father of our democracy, a tireless champion for social justice upon Africanist, a distinguished state man who was played a defining role in shaping Kenya's modern political history. We will definitely miss him immensely. Kenya is today a better place because of his struggles, his sacrifices, his leadership and vision and courage. Once again, thank you all for standing with us and this most difficult time for our country. Thank you. Thank you, Kenya. Once again, on behalf of the committee, we stand our condolences to the government and people of Kenya for the loss of a great assessment on our Raihla Odinga, which caused guardians upon the people of Kenya. With that, give the floor to Zista, please. Thank you, Chair, for allowing us to come in on this. And we would like to convey our deepest sympathies for the people of Kenya on the passing of their Prime Minister. Distinguished delegates, from the perspective of an industry NGO focused on standards and safety, we would like to express our support for the interventions of the Cook Islands and the Marshall Islands. In brief, we are now working on the technical implementation of the support of the GHG measures across multiple committees and working groups. And there are risks to be a lack of coherence across these separate groups, which will ultimately lead to confusion and implementation. It is crucial to assure that these issues and the burden that it will then place on the Secretaryate, taken into account however the committee chooses to proceed. Thank you. Thank you, Zista. Thanks to all the delegates for this interesting discussion on this matter. Our none-two-mind summing up on this very, very important issue. But before then, I'd like a public Secretary General to make a statement before I summing up, as he please. Thank you, Mr. President. Thank you, Mr. Seniors. And I'd like to thank all those who have spoken for your comments. Just coming in here to address some of the questions which were raised in particular on the document held on. MEPC-ES 234. With regard to costs and the question from the UAE, many of you will recall, the Council 133-7 document which gave information on the measures we've taken in order to address multilingualism and more specifically translation of documents. And that showed that we are really working at capacity. And Council members and delegations who have also acknowledged the impact affect that this can have on the health of interpreters. The Council document on the budget exercising the reference to 1.8 million. That refers to interpretation, translation, subcontracted out to assist with the work that's in-house and the cost of travel for interpreters and other small costs. It does not include the full cost of the staff in the conference division who are also involved in this. That's between $7.8 million per annum. Sorry. That is pounds sterling. Not dollars. So a five-day meeting cost calculation, MSI of course is many, is about half a million pounds sterling. That is the real cost of a meeting. The budget I've given you uses the efficiencies we've made in terms of the contracts issued in order to offset various costs. And there's also the requirement for additional staff for the MED, marine environment division in order to meet expectations. And this budget is for five-day meetings. We take the comments of the Marshall Islands. For example, for an expanded eight-day meeting, then I regret to have to say that that would increase the budget of course. As to whether MEPC could have two meetings in an assembly year, logistically speaking, that is extremely difficult for the Secretary, not just given the resources, but also the need to prepare committee reports, including MEPC, for assembly, that is in keeping with the IMO convention. And that's why in assembly years there is only one meeting of MSI and of MEPC. Telling to the organization of meetings, then of course, if it's agreed, we will work to the best of our abilities to find place for meetings of such a subcommittee. But if you look at the meeting schedule for next year, it's pretty full. And we have to bear in mind the times required by subcommittees for them to submit the reports to the committees, to then give time to the conference division to coordinate the translation of the documents from consecutive meetings to give you time so that where possible there's a week between meetings of committees, subcommittees, council and assembly. And that's not counting inter-session meetings. Where we also have to produce documents for you. So we can do it, but I would ask you to take this into account. In terms of specific weeks when meetings of this future subcommittee would have to be agreed. Finally, with regard to comments from the delegation of Tuvalu, of course, the right of the committee to decide which meetings the voluntary fund applies to those resources in order to assist with the participation of delegates from those countries. I just emphasize that that remains voluntary and we depend on donations, contributions from other countries in order to be able to assist countries which request aid. It is not a part of the regular budget of the organisation. Thank you, sir. Thank you. It's a secretary general for additional information and updates on this issue.
